item.title: Flying Fijians among world's best as Nations Championship Trophy unveiled
item.description: Flying Fijians among world's best as Nations Championship Trophy unveiled

item.title: Blues on red alert for Reece Walsh's roving role
France face Senegal in World Cup clash
item.description: Third-ranked France will take on 15th-ranked Senegal in a highly anticipated FIFA World Cup Group I clash in less than two hours, with both sides looking to make a winning start to their World Cup campaign. France enters the match as one of the favourites to lift the World Cup trophy and are expected to rely on stars such as Kylian Mbappe, Ousmane Dembele and Aurelien Tchouameni.
item.content:encoded:
Third-ranked France will take on 15th-ranked Senegal in a highly anticipated FIFA World Cup Group I clash in less than two hours, with both sides looking to make a winning start to their World Cup campaign.

France enters the match as one of the favourites to lift the World Cup trophy and are expected to rely on stars such as Kylian Mbappe, Ousmane Dembele and Aurelien Tchouameni.

Senegal, meanwhile, could look to key players including Nicolas Jackson, Kalidou Koulibaly and Idrissa Gana Gueye as they aim to challenge one of the tournament favourites.

The Group I encounter is expected to play a key role in shaping the standings, with Iraq and Norway also competing in the group.

France will be looking to use their attacking firepower and tournament experience to secure all three points, while Senegal will rely on their pace, physicality and defensive organisation to frustrate the 2018 world champions.

Senegal will also be hoping to draw inspiration from their famous 1-0 victory over France at the 2002 FIFA World Cup, one of the biggest upsets in tournament history.

The France-Senegal match kicks off at 7am.

Meanwhile in other matches, Iraq will face Norway at 10am, Argentina will meet Algeria at 1pm while Austria will face Jordan at 4pm.

item.title: Argentina rally around Messi ahead of World Cup opener
item.description: Lionel Messi may not have faced the media but dominated a chunk of Argentina's press conferences ahead of ?their World Cup opener,

item.title: State Of Origin: Nawaqanitawase to debut on the wing, Tino back in camp
item.description: The Westpac NSW Blues have been dealt a blow with Panthers young gun Casey McLean ruled out after straining a quad muscle at train

item.title: Qera urges fans to rally behind Flying Fijians ahead of Nations Championship
item.description: Former Fiji Water Flying Fijians captain Akapusi Qera is calling on supporters to get behind the FIJI Water Flying Fijians as they prepare for the upcoming Nations Championship. Qera, who captained Fiji in 28 Test matches, the most by any captain in the nation's history, believes the tournament will provide plenty of excitement for players and fans alike.
item.content:encoded:
Former Fiji Water Flying Fijians captain Akapusi Qera is calling on supporters to get behind the FIJI Water Flying Fijians as they prepare for the upcoming Nations Championship.

Qera, who captained Fiji in 28 Test matches, the most by any captain in the nation's history, believes the tournament will provide plenty of excitement for players and fans alike.

With just 18 days remaining before Fiji's opening match against Wales at Cardiff City Stadium, the former Dean's Trophy-winning captain is encouraging fans to secure their tickets early to avoid disappointment.

A former student and captain of Suva Grammar School, Qera helped his team claim the Dean's Trophy in 2002.

Now based in England, he is expecting a thrilling contest when Fiji takes on Wales.

Qera says the opening fixture will be a great test for the Flying Fijians and an exciting spectacle for rugby fans around the world.

Fiji will face Wales on the 4th of July at Cardiff City Stadium in Wales, with kick-off scheduled for 6.10pm.

item.title: Elijah Just's double earns New Zealand a point against Iran
item.description: Elijah Just scored twice as New Zealand fought back to secure a 2-2 draw against Iran in their FIFA World Cup Group G clash.Just w

item.title: Uruguay fight back to earn 1-all draw against Saudi Arabia
item.description: Uruguay will take on Cape Verde next Monday at 10am before meeting Spain in their final pool match next Saturday at 12pm.
item.content:encoded: Uruguay will take on Cape Verde next Monday at 10am before meeting Spain in their final pool match next Saturday at 12pm.

item.title: Cam Roigard fires back at backlash over Blues clash incident
item.description: All Blacks halfback Cam Roigard has hit back at critics who accused him of feigning injury to give the Hurricanes an unfair advant
